Merge pull request #188 from Resilient-Labs/187-move-leaflettags-to-mapviewejs

fix(map): move Leaflet JS from footer to mapView.ejs
feat(toast): add toastify to all forms and replace native alerts w styled toast alerts on sign pages and map, edited mapView.js to showToast instead of alert

**Related Issue(s):**
Closes #178

**Branch:** 178-integrate-toastifyjs

**What’s Included:**
- Integrated ToastifyJS across Sign-In, Sign-Up, and Map forms
- Replaced all native HTML5 validation pop-ups and alert() calls with styled toast messages
- Added public/js/toast.js to globally handle URL-based toasts and intercept form validation failures
- Updated views/partials/footer.ejs so that any req.flash messages render as Toastify toasts on every page
- Modified public/mapView(main).js to call showToast(...) instead of alert(...) for both form validation and server errors
- Adjusted views/signin.ejs, views/signup.ejs, and views/mapView.ejs to add novalidate on <form> and required attributes on inputs

**Notes for Reviewers:**
- Verify that Sign-In and Sign-Up pages no longer show native browser pop-ups, and instead show toast notifications on validation errors/success
- Confirm that the Map “Submit Alert” form uses showToast(…) for missing fields, geocoding failures, and successful submissions
- Make sure existing map pins still render correctly (if there’s any regression, it may require a separate frontend fix)
- No backend logic was touched, except adding 1 liner for middleware on server.js, only front-end files were updated. If anything breaks server-side, it should be unrelated to toast integration.

-  **May need to add a second fix ticket to add toast alerts and make sure they show up on feed.ejs and post.ejs pages, but rn signup/signin and map pages correctly display toast alerts**

**Title:** Feat(up-down-book): Added functionality for upvoting, downvoting and bookmarking posts

**Branch:** `181-add-logic`

What’s Included
- Upvoting, downvoting and bookmarking now works without reloading the page with the correct states.

This ticket addresses two issues on feed.ejs that affect user interaction and overall UX:
Upvote/Downvote Bug:
Users can currently upvote and downvote the same post, which should not be possible. Implement logic to prevent a user from casting both votes on the same post. Mirror the validation and state-checking behavior from post.ejs.
Bookmark UX Concern:
Currently, users can bookmark posts, but there’s no way to view only their bookmarked posts. If a filter toggle for bookmarks can’t be added to feed.ejs, the bookmarking feature should be removed entirely from both UI and backend handling to avoid confusion.

Currently, each alert on the feed.ejs (Alerts page) displays its type in camelCase (e.g., “suspiciousActivity”).
This ticket requires converting the displayed alert titles into readable, spaced-out English.

Tasks include:
- Create a simple JavaScript or EJS helper function to convert camelCase strings into normal English format.
Example: "suspiciousActivity" → "Suspicious Activity"

- Ensure the function applies dynamically to all alert titles rendered on the page.
- Test with various alert types to confirm consistent formatting.

(post → posts,  comment → comments)
Standardize all post and comment-related routes to follow RESTful naming and structure conventions. Scope includes:

- Rename all instances of singular routes:
    - /post → /posts
    - /comment → /comments
- Update route handlers, and frontend calls accordingly
- Ensure creation and deletion routes use RESTful structure:
    - POST /posts for creating a new post (formerly /newPost)
    - DELETE /posts/:id for deleting a post (formerly /deletePost)
- Apply the same logic to comments if needed:
    - POST /comments
    - DELETE /comments/:id
- Test and validate that all endpoints still function as expected
- Ensure consistent naming across the codebase (frontend/backend)
